general: Pin your favourite entries for quick access from the sidebar.
Use Collections to group related entries — trips, projects, books.
Add a reminder to any entry to revisit it later.
The command palette (Cmd+K) jumps you anywhere instantly.
note: Use headings to organise long notes — they show up in the outline.
Drop images straight into a note from your Photos library.
Try block quotes for capturing things people said.
voiceMemo: Record a quick thought on the go — Binder transcribes it for you.
Voice memos work great for ideas you'd lose otherwise.
list: Lists are perfect for packing, groceries, and trip planning.
Tap a finished item to cross it off — it stays for the record.
link: Save articles you want to read later with a rich preview.
Tag links by category to find them again.
event: Add a location and reminder to events you don't want to miss.
Attach photos after the event to build a memory.
recommendation: Rate places, films and finds so you can recommend them later.
Add a cover photo to make recommendations easy to spot.
locationPin: Drop a pin anywhere — bookmarks for the real world.
Add photos to a pin to remember why you saved it.
dailyMood: A daily check-in builds a picture of how you're really doing.
Look back over a week to spot what affects your mood.
photoGallery: Build a gallery for any moment — a trip, a meal, a project.
Drag photos to reorder them on macOS.
file: Attach any file — receipts, tickets, PDFs.
Files are searchable by name and live alongside your other entries.
collection: Group related entries into a single collection.
Collections work like folders — but smarter, with previews.

quotes.text: The life of every man is a diary in which he means to write one story, and writes another.
quotes.author: J.M. Barrie

quotes.text: I never travel without my diary. One should always have something sensational to read in the train.
quotes.author: Oscar Wilde

quotes.text: Journal writing is a voyage to the interior.
quotes.author: Christina Baldwin

quotes.text: Fill your paper with the breathings of your heart.
quotes.author: William Wordsworth

quotes.text: What a comfort is this journal. I tell myself to myself and throw the burden on my book and feel relieved.
quotes.author: Anne Lister

quotes.text: We do not learn from experience. We learn from reflecting on experience.
quotes.author: John Dewey
